extraction.one_line_summary: CAMPENAERTS attacked repeatedly with RIESEBEEK in final kilometers from breakaway, winning sprint after trading blows within 1km to go
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step sending three riders (CAMPENAERTS, WALSCHEID, WIŚNIOWSKI) to break reveals aggressive redundancy strategy maximizing stage win probability.
CAMPENAERTS and RIESEBEEK attacking each other rather than cooperating inside 1km demonstrates miscalculation allowing chasers to nearly bridge.
PUCCIO and GANNA riding front of peloton entire day despite no sprint threat suggests INEOS controlling tempo for GC protection.
HERMANS, CATALDO, ARNDT chasing as group of three shows coordinated multi-team effort to bridge, nearly successful within final kilometer.

rider_signals.CAMPENAERTS Victor: positive: first grand tour stage win after years attempting, proving sprint capability from small breakaway after sustained attacking
rider_signals.RIESEBEEK Oscar: positive: matched CAMPENAERTS attack-for-attack in finale, tactical error going 150m out enabled loss but shows strong form
rider_signals.BUCHMANN Emanuel: negative: abandoned after large early crash while sitting sixth GC, massive blow to BORA-hansgrohe stage race ambitions
rider_signals.MOLLEMA Bauke: negative: keen to chase for stage win per commentary but finished outside top five, suggests poor positioning or fading legs
rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: negative: DNS suggests illness or injury accumulating, significant loss for sprint train leadership
rider_signals.ARNDT Nikias: positive: led chasing group of five at finish, DSM showing consistent aggression in breakaway scenarios
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: neutral: rode peloton front entire day for tempo control rather than personal ambition, pure domestique work
